title: eriogonum flavum var. aquilinum
intro: Eriogonum flavum var. aquilinum, also known as Desert Buckwheat, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Desert Buckwheat include soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Desert Buckwheat can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as salves and creams.
